question_text: When a gas is heated, what happens to the movement of its particles?

options.label: A
options.text: The particles slow down and move more slowly

options.label: B
options.text: The particles speed up and move faster

options.label: C
options.text: The particles stay the same speed but change direction

explanation: When a gas is heated, the particles gain energy and move faster. This increased movement means more frequent and forceful collisions with container walls, which increases pressure. Temperature and particle speed are directly linked in the particle model.
